title: hieracium atratum subsp. floccidorsum
intro: Hieracium atratum subsp. floccidorsum, commonly known as a type of Hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium atratum subsp. floccidorsum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic actions of reducing pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress. The plant's health benefits include its potential to alleviate symptoms of eczema, acne, and other dermatological conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
parts: Hieracium atratum subsp. floccidorsum has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, and roots, which are used to create various remedies. The leaves of the Hieracium atratum subsp. floccidorsum plant are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. The flowers of this plant contain sesquiterpene lactones, including thiarubrine and thiasinin, which have been found to have antimicrobial and antitumor activities. The roots of Hieracium atratum subsp. floccidorsum are rich in alkaloids, including hieracine and leucosin, which have been reported to exhibit antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects.
